LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANT JAY TRISKO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DEFENDANT TRISKO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER A AND F ENGINEERING, INC. IS A CONTINUATION OF AJ PACHECO AND WHETHER DEFENDANT TRISKO'S DUTY OF CARE CONTINUED TO A AND F ENGINEERING, INC. AS A RESULT. (SEE PLAINTIFF'S UNDISPUTED FACTS 21-23 AND PACHECO DECLARATION 5.) DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO EVIDENCE CODE 452(D). PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO C.R.C. 3.1350(E)(4) AND EVIDENCE CODE 452(D)(1). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES E. MCGOVERN, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO SOUTHERN INSURANCE COMPANY AND FIRSTCOMP INSURANCE AGENCY, INC.'S CROSS COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT MCGOVERN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO CROSS-COMPLAINANTS SOUTHERN INSURANCE COMPANY AND FIRSTCOMP INSURANCE AGENCY IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT THE CROSS-COMPLAINANTS' RELIANCE ON THE 2007 WORKERS' COMPENSATION POLICY APPLICATION WAS UNJUSTIFIED AS A MATTER OF LAW. (SEE DEFENDANT'S UNDISPUTED FACTS 32, 34-39.) DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO EVIDENCE CODE 452(D). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANT JAMES E. MCGOVERN, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT MCGOVERN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT CANNOT ESTABLISH, AS A MATTER OF LAW, THAT THE PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH THE DAMAGES ELEMENT IN BOTH THE NEGLIGENCE AND BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON VALENTINE V. MEMBRILA INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. (2004) 118 CAL.APP.4TH 462. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO EVIDENCE CODE 452(D). PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO C.R.C. 3.1350(E)(4) AND EVIDENCE CODE 452(D)(1). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

Defendant James E. Mcgovern, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiff'S Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-12-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Elizabeth Williams, Esq. and Jonathan W. Black, Esq. of Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck LLP, Tel. 415.926.7600, for defendant and moving party James E. McGovern Insurance;
John N. Frye, Esq. of Galine Frye & Fitting, Tel. 650.345.8484, for plaintiff;
Barry R. Gammell, Esq. of Knapp Petersen Clarke, Tel. 818.547.5173, for CrossComplainants FirstComp Insurance Agency, Inc. & Southern Insurance Company;
Peter C. Catalanotti, Esq. of Manning & Kass, Tel. 415.217.6990 for defendant Trisko.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT JAMES E. MCGOVERN, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. Defendant McGovern's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant cannot establish, as a matter of law, that the Plaintiff fails to establish the damages element in both the negligence and breach of contract causes of action based on Valentine v. Membrila Insurance Services, Inc. (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 462. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted pursuant to Evidence Code 452(d). Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted pursuant to C.R.C. 3.1350(e)(4) and Evidence Code 452(d)(1). Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

MCGOVERN, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO SOUTHERN INSURANCE COMPANY AND FIRSTCOMP INSURANCE AGENCY, INC.'S CROSS COMPLAINT. Defendant McGovern's motion for summary judgment as to Cross-Complainants Southern Insurance Company and FirstComp Insurance Agency is denied. Defendant fails to establish that the Cross-Complainants' reliance on the 2007 Workers' Compensation policy application was unjustified as a matter of law. Off Calendar Mar-12-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Peter C. Catalanotti, Esq. of Manning & Kass, Tel. 415.217.6990 for defendant and moving party Trisko;
John N. Frye, Esq. of Galine Frye & Fitting, Tel. 650.345.8484, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Elizabeth Williams, Esq. and Jonathan W. Black, Esq. of Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck LLP, Tel. 415.926.7600, for defendant James E. McGovern Insurance;
Barry R. Gammell, Esq. of Knapp Petersen Clarke, Tel. 818.547.5173, for First Comp Southern Insurance.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT JAY TRISKO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant Trisko's motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiffs raise a triable issue of fact as to whether A and F Engineering, Inc. is a continuation of AJ Pacheco and whether Defendant Trisko's duty of care continued to A and F Engineering, Inc. as a result. (See Plaintiff's Undisputed Facts 21-23 and Pacheco declaration 5.) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted pursuant to Evidence Code 452(d). Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted pursuant to C.R.C. 3.1350(e)(4) and Evidence Code 452(d)(1). Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
